refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR used the phrase 'support achieving,' which can imply a solution approach. Replacing it with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's capability without prescribing how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single requirement: meeting the energy efficiency target defined by the customer.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish predictive maintenance to ensure energy-efficient operation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational policies may influence control parameters
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and control, which can be modularized independently from other system functions, supporting decoupling. Potential coupling arises if operational policies tightly constrain control logic, but this can be managed through interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and reliable energy measurement is feasible within system constraints
Operational policies allow for dynamic control adjustments
Energy efficiency targets are achievable through control and monitoring alone
Risk that measurement errors or delays could degrade control effectiveness
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for ensuring measurement accuracy and data integrity to support the control DP, enhancing robustness and independence.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Contains physical energy systems but monitoring and feedback control logic may be spread across other modules, causing moderate diffusion.
Primarily focused on remote control; energy monitoring is peripheral, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety may use energy data but does not own monitoring or control, resulting in low containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control due to its focus on energy management, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause some interface complexity.
If energy feedback control affects safety-critical functions, coordination with Safety Services is necessary to avoid conflicts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment issues arise due to cross-module dependencies, consider refining the DP to separate raw data acquisition (Vehicle) from monitoring and control logic (NGHE Solution) to improve modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target by providing the necessary monitoring and control functions to influence energy usage.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause some interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion with a containment score of 8 and low diffusion score of 2, indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measurement of energy_per_ton against the target E_target over the evaluation period, as well as monitoring the interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) for real-time data and control command exchange.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and logical, with well-defined interfaces and ownership, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to separate raw energy data acquisition (to be handled by the Vehicle module) from monitoring and feedback control logic (within NGHE Solution) to reduce interface complexity and improve modular clarity.
Ensure coordination mechanisms with Safety Services module if energy feedback control impacts safety-critical functions to avoid conflicts and maintain system safety.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, the recommended revisions highlight important improvements to reduce interface complexity and ensure safety coordination due to the high safety criticality. Refining the DP to separate raw energy data acquisition from monitoring and feedback control will improve modular clarity and reduce dependency risks. Additionally, ensuring coordination with the Safety Services module is critical to maintain system safety. These revisions should be addressed before proceeding to derive next-level sub-FRs to maintain a robust and safe design.

revise.why: To separate raw energy data acquisition (to be handled by the Vehicle module) from monitoring and feedback control logic (within NGHE Solution) to reduce interface complexity and improve modular clarity, and to ensure coordination mechanisms with Safety Services module for safety-critical functions.

assumptions: The current DP combines data acquisition and control logic, causing interface complexity.
Safety Services module may be impacted by energy feedback control actions.
open_risks: Interface complexity due to dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition.
Potential conflicts with Safety Services module affecting system safety.
evidence_needed: Verification of refined DP separation effectiveness in reducing interface complexity.
Validation of coordination mechanisms with Safety Services module to ensure safety.
